chapter_description: Newton's laws, friction, circular motion, work-energy theorem, conservation of momentum

questions.text: Which of Newton's laws explains why a passenger lurches forward when a bus stops suddenly?
questions.explanation: First law of motion — law of inertia: a body continues in its state of rest or uniform motion unless acted upon by an external force.

questions.text: A block of 5 kg on a frictionless surface is pushed with 20 N force. What is the acceleration?
A conical pendulum has string length L and makes angle θ with the vertical. Find the time period.
questions.explanation: T = 2π√(L cos θ / g). Resolving forces: T sin θ = mω²r and T cos θ = mg.

questions.text: A 10 kg block is accelerated at 3 m/s². What is the net force (in Newtons) acting on it?
A ball is thrown vertically upward with velocity 20 m/s. Find the maximum height reached (in meters). Take g = 10 m/s².
A 2 kg block slides down a 30° incline with coefficient of kinetic friction μ = 0.2. Find the acceleration (in m/s²). Take g = 10 m/s².
